questions.text: On the surface of _, the mRNA and tRNA interact to translate the information from genes into a specific protein.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: DNA, or deoxyribonucleic acid, is a molecule that carries the genetic instructions necessary for the growth, development, functioning, and reproduction of all known living organisms and many viruses. It is often described as the "blueprint of life." DNA is a long, double-stranded helical molecule made up of four basic building blocks called nucleotides. The structure of DNA is a double helix, with two long chains (strands) of nucleotides running in opposite directions and connected by hydrogen bonds between complementary base pairs. There are four types of nucleotides in DNA, each characterized by a nitrogenous base.

A chromosome is a thread-like structure composed of DNA and proteins that carries genetic information. Chromosomes are found in the nucleus of eukaryotic cells and, in the context of cellular division, also in the nucleoid region of prokaryotic cells. Chromosomes are essential for the transmission of genetic material from one generation of cells or organisms to the next.

A ribosome is a cellular structure that functions as the site of protein synthesis in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells. It plays a crucial role in translating genetic information from messenger RNA (mRNA) into functional proteins. Ribosomes are composed of two subunits, and they are typically found in the cytoplasm of the cell.

A nucleosome is a basic structural unit of DNA packaging in eukaryotic cells. It consists of DNA wrapped around a core of histone proteins. Nucleosomes play a fundamental role in organizing and compacting the long strands of DNA, allowing it to fit within the confined space of the cell nucleus.

questions.explanation: A ribosome is a cellular structure that functions as the site of protein synthesis in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells. It plays a crucial role in translating genetic information from messenger RNA (mRNA) into functional proteins. Ribosomes are composed of two subunits, and they are typically found in the cytoplasm of the cell.

questions.text: In an amino acid, in which the R group is hydrogen, the amino acid will be:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Alanine is a non-essential amino acid, meaning that the human body can synthesize it and it is not strictly required from the diet. It is one of the 20 standard amino acids that make up proteins. The chemical structure of alanine consists of an amino group (-NH₂), a carboxyl group (-COOH), and a side chain methyl group (-CH₃). Its chemical formula is C₃H₇NO₂.

Glycine is the simplest amino acid, both structurally and chemically, among the 20 standard amino acids that serve as the building blocks of proteins. It is a non-essential amino acid, meaning that the human body can synthesize it, and it is not strictly required to be obtained from the diet. Glycine plays various roles in the body, beyond its incorporation into proteins.

Leucine is one of the 20 standard amino acids that serve as the building blocks of proteins. It is an essential amino acid, meaning that it cannot be synthesized by the human body and must be obtained through the diet. Leucine is classified as a branched-chain amino acid (BCAA) due to its aliphatic side chain with a branched structure. It plays crucial roles in protein synthesis, energy production, and various physiological processes.

Valine is one of the 20 standard amino acids, which are the building blocks of proteins. It is classified as an essential amino acid, meaning that the human body cannot synthesize it and must obtain it through the diet. Valine is a branched-chain amino acid (BCAA), and along with leucine and isoleucine, it plays important roles in protein synthesis, energy production, and various physiological functions.

options.explanation: A hydroxyl group is a functional group (-OH) consisting of an oxygen atom covalently bonded to a hydrogen atom. It is commonly found in organic compounds and is the defining feature of alcohols. The hydroxyl group imparts specific chemical properties to the compounds that contain it.

An amino group is a functional group in organic chemistry that contains a nitrogen atom bonded to two hydrogen atoms. The amino group is a crucial component of amino acids, the building blocks of proteins, and is commonly found in a variety of organic compounds.

An aldehydic group, or aldehyde group, is a functional group in organic chemistry characterized by a carbonyl group (a carbon-oxygen double bond, C=O) with a hydrogen atom (H) directly attached to the carbonyl carbon. The general formula for an aldehyde group is RCHO, where R represents an organic group.

The general formula for an aldehyde group is RCHO, where R represents an organic group. A ketonic group, or ketone group, is a functional group in organic chemistry characterized by a carbonyl group (a carbon-oxygen double bond, C=O) with two organic groups attached to the carbonyl carbon. The general formula for a ketone group is RCOR', where R and R' represent different organic groups.

options.explanation: Ribose is a type of sugar and a component of RNA (ribonucleic acid), which is one of the two major types of nucleic acids found in cells. The other major nucleic acid is DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid). Ribose is a pentose sugar, meaning it has five carbon atoms in its molecular structure.

Erythrose is a four-carbon sugar, specifically a ketotetrose. It is classified as a monosaccharide, which means it is a simple sugar that cannot be broken down into smaller sugars through hydrolysis. The name "erythrose" is derived from the Greek word "erythros," meaning red, although the exact reason for this name is not entirely clear.

D-Glucoheptose is a sugar that can be used as an alternative to sucrose in the food industry. It is obtained by hydrolysis of inulin, which is a complex carbohydrate that consists of chains of fructose molecules with terminal d-glucose residues.

Galactose is a monosaccharide sugar that is often found in combination with glucose to form the disaccharide lactose, which is commonly found in milk and dairy products. It is one of the building blocks of carbohydrates. When consumed in the diet, lactose is broken down into its constituent sugars, glucose, and galactose, by an enzyme called lactase, which is produced in the small intestine. These individual sugars can then be absorbed into the bloodstream.

options.explanation: An aldose is a type of monosaccharide, which is a simple sugar. The term "aldose" specifically refers to a monosaccharide that contains an aldehyde functional group. An aldehyde group consists of a carbon atom double-bonded to an oxygen atom and single-bonded to a hydrogen atom. The general chemical formula for an aldose is Cn(H2O)n. A hexose is a monosaccharide that contains six carbon atoms. Glucose is an example of an aldohexose, as it is a six-carbon sugar with an aldehyde group. The chemical formula for glucose is C6H12O6.

A furanose is a type of carbohydrate that is a five-membered ring structure formed when a monosaccharide undergoes a process called furanose formation. A pentose is a monosaccharide with five carbon atoms. When these concepts are combined, you get a furanose pentose sugar. Ribose and deoxyribose are examples of furanose pentose sugars. They are important components of nucleotides, which are the building blocks of nucleic acids like RNA and DNA. The furanose ring structure in these sugars is derived from the cyclization of an open-chain form.

A ketose is a type of monosaccharide, a simple sugar, that contains a ketone functional group. The term "ketone" refers to a carbonyl group where a carbon atom is double-bonded to an oxygen atom within the carbon skeleton of the sugar. A hexose is a monosaccharide with six carbon atoms. An example of a ketose hexose sugar is fructose.

A pyranose is a type of carbohydrate that forms a six-membered ring structure when a monosaccharide undergoes a process called pyranose formation. A pentose, on the other hand, is a monosaccharide with five carbon atoms. Combining these concepts, you get a pyranose pentose sugar. Ribose is an example of a pyranose pentose sugar. Ribose is a five-carbon sugar that plays a crucial role in the structure of RNA (ribonucleic acid). options.explanation: Methionine is an essential amino acid, meaning that the body cannot produce it and must obtain it from the diet. It is one of the building blocks of proteins. The three primary pyrimidine bases are cytosine (C), thymine (T, found in DNA), and uracil (U, found in RNA).

The energy released during catabolic reactions is often captured in the form of adenosine triphosphate (ATP), which serves as the primary energy currency of cells.

Anabolic reactions are metabolic processes that involve the synthesis of complex molecules from simpler ones, requiring an input of energy. These reactions are responsible for building larger molecules and structures essential for the growth, maintenance, and function of living organisms. questions.text: The attraction between water molecules and cell wall of xylem is termed as:

options.explanation: Cohesion refers to the tendency of molecules of the same kind to stick together. This phenomenon is particularly noticeable in liquids, where the molecules are in close proximity.Water molecules are attracted to each other due to hydrogen bonding. In a water molecule, the oxygen atom is more electronegative than the hydrogen atoms, creating a partial negative charge near the oxygen and partial positive charges near the hydrogens. This polarity allows water molecules to form hydrogen bonds with each other.
Surface tension is a measure of the cohesive forces at the surface of a liquid. It is the result of the tendency of liquid molecules to minimize their surface area.

Adhesion is the tendency of dissimilar particles or surfaces to cling to one another. It's the force of attraction that causes different substances to stick together. Adhesion is the opposite of cohesion, which refers to the tendency of like particles or surfaces to stick together.Water molecules exhibit adhesion when they "stick" to other surfaces, such as the walls of a tube or the surface of a solid. Adhesion, along with cohesion, contributes to capillary action, where liquids move against gravity in narrow spaces. It also influences the behavior of liquids in containers, such as meniscus formation in a graduated cylinder.

Imbibition is a process in which a substance absorbs water, causing it to increase in volume. This phenomenon is particularly observed in plant biology, where imbibition plays a crucial role in processes like seed germination and the movement of water through plant tissues.

questions.text: The covalent bond between two monosaccharide subunits is called?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A phosphodiester bond is a type of covalent bond that forms the backbone of nucleic acid polymers, such as DNA and RNA. These bonds connect the individual nucleotide units, providing structural stability to the nucleic acid molecule.n the process of nucleic acid polymerization, a phosphodiester bond is formed through a condensation reaction (dehydration synthesis) between the phosphate group of one nucleotide and the hydroxyl group (specifically, the 3'-OH group) of the sugar molecule of another nucleotide.

A peptide bond is a type of chemical bond that forms between two amino acids during protein synthesis. It is a covalent bond that links the carboxyl group (-COOH) of one amino acid with the amino group (-NH2) of another amino acid, resulting in the formation of a dipeptide. This process occurs through a condensation reaction, also known as dehydration synthesis, where a water molecule is eliminated as the bond forms.

An ionic bond is a type of chemical bond that occurs between two atoms when there is a significant difference in their electronegativities. Electronegativity is a measure of an atom's ability to attract and hold onto electrons. In an ionic bond, one atom, typically a metal, donates one or more electrons to another atom, typically a non-metal. As a result, one atom becomes positively charged (cation), and the other becomes negatively charged (anion).

A glycosidic bond is a type of covalent bond that links a carbohydrate molecule to another group, which can be another carbohydrate or a non-carbohydrate moiety. This bond forms through a condensation reaction, similar to the peptide bond in proteins. In a condensation reaction, a molecule of water is eliminated as the bond forms.

questions.explanation: A glycosidic bond is a type of covalent bond that links a carbohydrate molecule to another group, which can be another carbohydrate or a non-carbohydrate moiety. This bond forms through a condensation reaction, similar to the peptide bond in proteins. In a condensation reaction, a molecule of water is eliminated as the bond forms.

questions.text: Which of following cannot be hydrolyzed?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Polysaccharides are complex carbohydrates composed of long chains of monosaccharide units linked together by glycosidic bonds. They play various essential roles in living organisms, serving as storage molecules, structural components, and energy sources. Polysaccharides are found in both plants and animals.

Monosaccharides are the simplest form of carbohydrates and are often referred to as simple sugars. They are the building blocks of more complex carbohydrates like disaccharides and polysaccharides. Monosaccharides are composed of carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen atoms in a ratio of 1:2:1, and they serve as a primary source of energy for living organisms. The most common monosaccharides include glucose, fructose, and galactose.

Oligosaccharides are carbohydrates composed of a small number of monosaccharide units, typically 3 to 10 sugar molecules. They represent an intermediate stage of complexity between monosaccharides (single sugar units) and polysaccharides (long chains of sugar units). Oligosaccharides play various roles in biological systems, including serving as energy sources, cell recognition molecules, and components of larger structures like glycoproteins and glycolipids.

Sucrose is a disaccharide, which means it is composed of two monosaccharide units joined by a glycosidic bond. Specifically, sucrose is made up of one molecule of glucose and one molecule of fructose. The glycosidic bond linking these two monosaccharides is formed between the anomeric carbon of glucose and the hydroxyl group of fructose.

questions.text: Animal starch is called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Cellulose is a complex carbohydrate, or polysaccharide, consisting of a linear chain of several hundred to over ten thousand β-D-glucose units linked together by β-1,4-glycosidic bonds. It is a major component of plant cell walls and provides structural support to plant cells.

Agar is a gelatinous substance derived from certain types of red algae, primarily of the genera Gelidium and Gracilaria. It is widely used in various applications, including the preparation of solid culture media for microbiological work, as a vegetarian alternative to gelatin in food, and in scientific research and industry.

Glycogen is a complex carbohydrate, specifically a polysaccharide, that serves as a form of energy storage in animals and humans. It is a branched polymer made up of glucose units linked together by glycosidic bonds. Structurally, glycogen is similar to amylopectin, a component of starch found in plants.

Chitin is a complex polysaccharide that is a major structural component in the exoskeletons of arthropods (such as insects, spiders, and crustaceans), as well as the cell walls of fungi and some algae. It is one of the most abundant biopolymers in nature.Chitin is composed of linear chains of N-acetylglucosamine (GlcNAc) monomers, which are linked together by β-1,4-glycosidic bonds. The chemical structure of chitin is similar to that of cellulose, which is found in the cell walls of plants, but with the replacement of glucose by N-acetylglucosamine.

questions.text: It is not a monosaccharide:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Fructose is a monosaccharide, a simple sugar, and it is one of the three most important dietary monosaccharides, along with glucose and galactose. It has the same chemical formula as glucose (C6H12O6), but the arrangement of atoms is different, making it an isomer of glucose. Fructose is commonly found in fruits, honey, and certain vegetables, and it is often referred to as "fruit sugar."

Glucose and fructose are both monosaccharide sugars, meaning they are simple sugars composed of a single sugar unit. They are isomers, meaning they have the same molecular formula (C6H12O6) but differ in their structural arrangements. These sugars are important sources of energy for the body and are commonly found in various foods.

Sucrose is a common disaccharide, meaning it is composed of two monosaccharide sugar units. Specifically, sucrose is made up of one molecule of glucose and one molecule of fructose, joined together by a glycosidic bond. It is often referred to as table sugar and is the most widely used sugar in food and beverage production.
